title: Implement v2 fallback-safe gated proposal kernel
description: Replace the three-dial adaptive mapping in TextSideProposalBuilder with the single evidence-gated mixture anchored to the core learnable proposal softmax(p1); pseudo-item mass stays exactly the core value and u never modifies it. Legacy v1 path stays selectable for ablation arms.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN kernel_version=v2 is configured THEN proposal equals p_core(pseudo)*e_pseudo + (1-p_core(pseudo))*[(1-g)*renorm(p_core)+g*c_h] with g=g_max*clamp(u_tilde,0,1) and no new learnable modules beyond core p1; WHEN is_disliked_item is false THEN proposal reduces to (1-g)*p_core+g*c_h; WHEN ablation modes run under v2 THEN global_p forces g==0 and text_anchor_only forces g==g_max and u_shuffle permutes u_tilde across batch users; WHEN kernel_version=v1 THEN legacy behavior is unchanged and existing text-side tests still pass; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md:43
review_initial_requirements: Check gradients flow to p1 through both mixture branches and the pseudo-item coordinate is taken from p_core, never from u.
review_regression_requirements: Verify spec section 1.2 decisions 1, 3, and 4 are implemented in the v2 kernel path; verify c_h down-weights zero-text items via completeness; verify no temperature or popularity_mix or pseudo_mass coupling to u remains in the v2 path; decision 2 null-calibration wiring is owned by SPRINT-02.
notes: source_doc:docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md; decision:pseudo-mass-frozen-to-core; risk:medium v1 ablation arms must stay runnable; picked_reason:P0 first executable row and prerequisite for all later v2 gates; boundary_fix:decision2-null-calibration-owned-by-SPRINT-02; evidence:python -m unittest tests.test_text_side_proposal tests.test_proposal_wiring tests.test_repo_repro_contract => 23 tests OK; evidence:python -m unittest tests.test_text_side_proposal tests.test_build_agreement_null_curves tests.test_proposal_wiring tests.test_repo_repro_contract tests.test_build_text_side_embeddings_script => 26 tests OK

title: Build length-matched agreement null calibration
description: Add an offline builder for per-dataset agreement null curves mu_null(L) and sigma_null(L) from frozen text banks, and wire u_tilde=(A_h-mu_null(L))/(k*sigma_null(L)) into v2 context encoding.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN scripts/build_agreement_null_curves.py runs on a dataset directory THEN it writes a null-curve artifact keyed by history-length bins with fixed seed and records the bank hash into protocol metadata; WHEN the same inputs are rebuilt THEN the artifact is byte-stable; WHEN encode_history_context runs under v2 THEN u_tilde uses the length-matched null lookup and a random pseudo-history scores u_tilde near 0; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md:59
review_initial_requirements: Check null curves are computed only from the frozen bank with no user interaction data and no target leakage.
review_regression_requirements: Verify length bins cover observed history lengths for all four datasets; verify defaults match spec initial values k=2.0 g_max=0.5 tau0=0.2.

title: Prove g==0 core equivalence before any remote launch
description: Add the equivalence suite showing the v2 kernel with g==0 reproduces core PreferGrow exactly; this is the hard gate that must pass before main-table retrains launch.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN g==0 THEN forward corruption rows and score_entropy terms and reverse_prob_ratio outputs and sample_nonpreference distributions match the core AdaptiveWise graph within numerical tolerance given identical p1; WHEN a short smoke training run executes with v2 g==0 THEN its loss and metric curve matches a same-seed core run within noise and the comparison is archived under docs/reports/data; WHEN this suite fails THEN SPRINT-05 must not start; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md:70
review_initial_requirements: Check the comparison is numeric against the real core code path, not a mocked or re-derived formula.
review_regression_requirements: Verify Prop 6 preconditions hold in code: pseudo mass from p_core, identical state space including the pseudo coordinate, proposal threading through all four kernel call sites at graph_lib.py:1323.

title: Run Gate 0 production-bank u_tilde diagnostics
description: On the remote production sentence-t5-xl banks compute null curves and real-user u_tilde distributions for Steam, ML1M, Beauty, ATG and issue the Gate 0 go or no-go verdict.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN diagnostics run on production banks THEN per-dataset median and distribution of real-user u_tilde are written to docs/reports/data with a dated report; WHEN Gate 0 is evaluated THEN it passes only if abs(median u_tilde on ML1M) < 0.5 and median u_tilde on Steam and Beauty exceeds the ML1M median; WHEN the criterion fails THEN the report states the revised hypothesis and main-table retrains stay blocked; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md:94
review_initial_requirements: Check diagnostics use regenerated production banks, not local legacy embeddings.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the verdict quotes the spec Gate 0 criterion and records the resulting decision to open or keep blocking SPRINT-05.

title: Diagnose Gate0 ML1M null-calibration failure on production banks
description: Using the same production sentence-t5-xl banks as Gate0, build a component-level diagnostic that explains why ML1M stayed far above the null point and formalizes the next-step path before any v2 main-table retrain is reopened.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the diagnostic runs on production banks THEN it writes a dated report under docs/reports/data summarizing per-dataset distributions of raw agreement, matched mu_null and sigma_null, history length, completeness, history_reliability, u_tilde, and g; WHEN ML1M is compared with Steam and Beauty THEN the report states whether the Gate0 fail is primarily explained by raw agreement residual above mu_null, null-curve spread, or another observed mismatch and cites the evidence; WHEN the report is finalized THEN it explicitly recommends either calibration repair first or frozen-claim downgrade before reopening SPRINT-05; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md:98
review_initial_requirements: Check the diagnostic reads the same production-bank artifacts as Gate0 and reports raw agreement alongside matched null stats, not only final u_tilde.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the decision section cites the actual Gate0 fail artifacts and does not reopen SPRINT-05 without a repaired calibration or an explicit claim downgrade path.

title: Audit Gate0 null-curve spread/scaling repair path before retrains
description: Before any v2 main-table retrain is reopened, audit whether the Gate0 ML1M failure can be repaired by null-curve spread or scaling changes on the existing production-bank path, and formalize the main-path decision if no plausible global repair exists.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the calibration audit runs on the current Gate0 artifacts or regenerated null-curve candidates THEN it writes a dated report under docs/reports/data/2026-07-02-gate0 comparing candidate spread or k or floor repairs against the current ML1M/Steam/Beauty medians; WHEN the report is finalized THEN it states whether any single repair directionally satisfies abs(median_u_tilde(ML1M)) < 0.5 while preserving the Steam/Beauty ordering gates without reopening main-table retrains; WHEN no plausible repair is supported THEN it records that the stronger main-path claim should downgrade instead of reopening SPRINT-05; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md:98
review_initial_requirements: Check the audit uses the same Gate0 production-bank path and explicitly cites which spread or scaling knob is being tested, rather than silently changing unrelated model behavior.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the decision section keeps SPRINT-05 blocked unless a repaired calibration path has explicit evidence from the audit artifacts.

title: Freeze post-audit main-path boundary against the original design
description: Compare the original 2026-06-24 text-side design and the 2026-07-02 fallback-safe sprint spec against the full Gate0 evidence chain, and freeze a dated decision memo stating how far the project has deviated, whether it can still return to the main path, and which next moves remain inside the approved design boundary.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the decision memo is finalized THEN it writes a dated Chinese report under docs/reports/data/2026-07-02-gate0 that explicitly answers what the original idea was, what Gate0 and FOLLOWUP-01/FOLLOWUP-02 actually showed, whether the current result matches the idea, and if not why; WHEN the main-path judgment is stated THEN the report must distinguish what still counts as in-design deeper repair versus what would be a real design departure; WHEN the conclusion is recorded THEN it keeps SPRINT-05 blocked unless a new Gate0-passing repair path is explicitly evidenced; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-24-usdpd-text-side-kernel-design.md:25
review_initial_requirements: Check the memo cites the original design doc, the fallback-safe sprint spec, and the dated Gate0 artifacts rather than freehand recollection.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the conclusion answers the user's core decision question directly: what the idea was, what the result is, whether it met expectation, and whether the project can still return to the main path without changing the scientific question.

title: Design in-boundary deeper Gate0 repair options before any retrain reopen
description: Using the Gate0 failure chain and the newly frozen boundary memo, design the next repair options that still stay inside the original history-only fallback-safe question, and rank which one should be tried first before SPRINT-05 can ever reopen.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the design memo is finalized THEN it writes a dated Chinese report under docs/reports/data/2026-07-02-gate0 enumerating concrete deeper-than-global repair families that remain inside the approved boundary; WHEN the options are compared THEN the memo must rank them by closeness to the original design, expected ability to fix Gate0, implementation surface, and risk of turning into a different research question; WHEN the recommendation is recorded THEN it either selects one in-boundary repair as the next implementation target or explicitly recommends claim downgrade if no plausible in-boundary repair remains; ref: docs/reports/data/2026-07-02-gate0/gate0_original_design_return_assessment_zh.md:1
review_initial_requirements: Check every proposed repair keeps the history-only fallback-safe kernel boundary explicit and does not silently reopen retraining.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the memo names at least one recommended next repair target or honestly concludes that downgrade is the only remaining boundary-consistent path.

title: Run production-bank text-utility diagnostic for the Gate0 construct check
description: Supersedes the Family A spread repair per the 2026-07-03 analysis: the Gate0 failure is a construct error in the gate signal, not a spread-scaling error. Compute the frozen train-only text-utility statistic U_ds on l20 production banks for all four datasets per spec section 7.3.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the diagnostic runs on l20 THEN it computes U_ds per spec 7.3 (4000 sampled train transitions, seed 7, 100 popularity-sampled negatives as the primary statistic, uniform negatives and coherence-quartile breakdown as diagnostics) for ML1M, Steam, Beauty, ATG from production t5-xl banks and regenerated paper_raw_v1 train splits; WHEN the report is written THEN it archives a dated artifact under docs/reports/data/2026-07-02-gate0 recording bank hash and split hash per dataset; WHEN the numbers are in THEN the row records the Gate 0-v2 inputs without deciding pass or fail (decision belongs to FOLLOWUP-07); ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md:182
review_initial_requirements: Check the estimator matches spec 7.3 exactly (sample size, seed, negative sampling law) and reads only train splits, never val or test.
review_regression_requirements: Verify U_ds values are computed from production banks, not local legacy embeddings; verify popularity negatives use train next-item frequencies.

title: Implement utility-gated g per spec amendment 7.2
description: Add the frozen dataset-level factor phi(U_ds) to the v2 gate so g = g_max * phi(U_ds) * clamp(u_tilde,0,1), loading U_ds from the FOLLOWUP-05 artifact; sync the paper gate equation. No learnable modules; phi parameters are frozen at (0.70, 0.10).
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the v2 gate computes g THEN it multiplies the frozen phi(U_ds) dataset constant with the existing clamp(u_tilde,0,1) user factor and g_max, with U_ds loaded from a dated artifact recording bank hash; WHEN phi is evaluated THEN it equals clip((0.70-U)/0.10,0,1) with no tunable reparameterization; WHEN the g==0 equivalence suite reruns THEN it still passes unchanged since g=0 forcing is unaffected; WHEN paper/main.tex is synced THEN the method gate equation and calibration narrative match spec 7.2 and 7.5; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md:169

acceptance_criteria: WHEN production U_ds values exist THEN the three spec 7.4 conditions are evaluated verbatim (ML1M is max; phi(U_ML1M) <= 0.2; phi >= 0.5 on at least two of Steam, Beauty, ATG); WHEN all three hold THEN SPRINT-05 blocked_by is cleared to launch with the two-factor gate and the verdict is archived under docs/reports/data/2026-07-02-gate0; WHEN any condition fails THEN a dated Family D downgrade memo is written by 2026-07-07 and no further gate repair is attempted; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md:186
review_initial_requirements: Check the criterion is applied exactly as frozen in spec 7.4 with no post-hoc threshold adjustment.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the verdict artifact quotes the three conditions with their margins and the resulting SPRINT-05 decision.

acceptance_criteria: WHEN the freeze memo is written THEN it fixes the abstract-level claim sentences, the forbidden-claim list (spec 4.6 inherited), and the U_ds-vs-v1-outcome inverse-ordering table as a headline analysis, archived dated under docs/reports/data; WHEN the SPRINT-05 validation results later arrive THEN wording changes are allowed only as an explicit documented upgrade per spec 8.2 rule 3, never silently; WHEN the deadline passes THEN the frozen wording is the operative paper baseline regardless of pending runs; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md:206

title: Run per-user utility-vs-harm analysis within datasets
description: Spec 8.3 reinforcement 1: compute per-user train-only text utility from production banks and correlate with per-user v1-vs-core performance deltas within ML1M (required) and at least one more dataset, using remote v1 artifacts or frozen v1 checkpoints for per-user evaluation.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the analysis runs THEN it produces within-dataset scatter or bucket tables of per-user text utility versus per-user v1-vs-core delta with thousands of users per dataset, archived dated under docs/reports/data; WHEN the relation is summarized THEN the report states whether the inverse utility-harm ordering holds at user level and with what rank statistic; WHEN per-user v1 eval dumps are unavailable THEN per-user evaluation is regenerated from frozen v1 best checkpoints instead of being skipped; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md:222
review_initial_requirements: Check utility uses train-split transitions only and the per-user deltas come from the frozen v1 checkpoint generation, not new tuning.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the report distinguishes correlation strength per dataset and does not overstate a causal claim.

title: Run v2 control arms on Beauty and Steam
description: Run u_shuffle, text_anchor_only, and global_p control arms under the v2 kernel on Beauty and Steam and assemble the control comparison table.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN control arms complete THEN a control table reports val p5 and test base p2 p5 p10 per arm alongside v2 full; WHEN u_shuffle is compared to v2 full THEN the table states explicitly whether shuffling degrades ranking; WHEN global_p is compared to core THEN it serves as the in-training core-equivalence check; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md:86
review_initial_requirements: Check all arms share identical splits, banks, and selector with the v2 main runs.
review_regression_requirements: Verify conclusions in the report do not overstate beyond the table and both datasets are covered.
notes: source_doc:docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md; deadline:2026-07-14

title: Rerun Beauty token-dropout corruption chain under v2
description: Rerun the standard token_dropout corruption evidence chain under the v2 kernel reusing existing corrupted banks, and adapt backoff diagnostics to v2 quantities g and u_tilde in place of v1 temperature and pseudo-mass readouts.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN corrupted-bank runs complete for v2 full and text_anchor_only and u_shuffle THEN clean-vs-corrupted degradation deltas are tabulated per arm; WHEN backoff diagnostics run THEN they report clean-vs-corrupted shifts of g and u_tilde bucketed by clean u_tilde; WHEN diagnostics scripts are adapted THEN v1 report compatibility is not silently broken; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md:86
review_initial_requirements: Check corrupted banks are the existing frozen artifacts and are not regenerated with different corruption seeds.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the backoff table supports or refutes the adaptive fallback claim and the outcome is recorded either way.
notes: source_doc:docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md; reuse:corrupted_banks; deadline:2026-07-16

title: Run u-composition ablation arms on Beauty
description: Compare calibrated-agreement-only (v2 main) against +completeness, +history-reliability, and legacy full_u arms on Beauty.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the four arms complete THEN a composition table reports selected-checkpoint val and test metrics per arm; WHEN legacy full_u runs THEN it uses the preserved v1 path unchanged; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md:59
review_initial_requirements: Check arms differ only in the u composition and share all other config.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the table is usable as the paper u-composition ablation without re-deriving numbers.
notes: source_doc:docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md; droppable:if-time-runs-out; deadline:2026-07-18

title: Run 3-way injection comparison under v2
description: Run encoder and loss injection modes against kernel injection on Beauty and Steam under v2 to feed the regime-analysis section. The paper claims safety-uniqueness of kernel injection, not global dominance.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the three injection modes complete on both datasets THEN a 3-way table with the shared selector is produced; WHEN results are written into the paper THEN wording follows spec section 4.4 with kernel as the only injection site expressing the reduction guarantee; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md:123
review_initial_requirements: Check encoder and loss modes reuse the anchor proposal semantics defined in model/text_side.py injection modes.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the paper section does not claim kernel is consistently best when the table does not support it.
notes: source_doc:docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md; droppable:if-time-runs-out; deadline:2026-07-18

title: Run 3-seed stability study for the v2 main table
description: Add two extra seeds per dataset for the v2 main-table runs and report mean and std of deltas vs core.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN seed runs complete THEN per-dataset mean and std of delta val and test p2 ndcg10 are tabulated; WHEN any sign flips across seeds THEN the paper table flags it explicitly; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md:86
review_initial_requirements: Check seed runs reuse identical configs except the seed.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the paper main table cites mean and std sourced from this study.
notes: source_doc:docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md; droppable:if-time-runs-out; deadline:2026-07-18

title: Freeze Gate 2 claim strength decision
description: Assemble the v2 main table plus controls, choose the strong or medium or weak exit per spec, and freeze final claim wording for the paper.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN all P0 and P1 artifacts are in THEN a dated Gate 2 report selects exactly one exit (strong: fallback-safe plus positive anchors; medium: safety-without-loss; weak: frozen-claim fallback) with the evidence table inline; WHEN the exit is chosen THEN the paper claim sentences are frozen and stronger claims are forbidden in later edits; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md:94
review_initial_requirements: Check the decision uses only dated artifacts under docs/reports/data.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the frozen wording is consistent with spec sections 4.2 and 4.6 and the chosen exit.
notes: source_doc:docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md; gate:GATE-2; deadline:2026-07-14

title: Fill paper placeholders from v2 artifacts
description: Regenerate paper figures and tables from v2 artifacts and replace every placeholder in paper/main.tex. Numbers may come only from real run artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN figures regenerate THEN the null-calibration figure, main table, backoff mechanism figure, and calibration buckets are produced from dated artifacts; WHEN placeholders are replaced THEN no pending macro instances remain and every number cites a docs/reports/data artifact in a tex comment; WHEN the Gate 2 exit is medium or weak THEN abstract and contribution wording are downgraded per the frozen decision; ref: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md:127
review_initial_requirements: Check no placeholder is filled from memory or extrapolation instead of an artifact.
review_regression_requirements: Verify the compiled PDF has no pending markers and respects the spec 4.6 forbidden-claim list.
notes: source_doc:docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md; deadline:2026-07-21-abstract-2026-07-28-full

title: Review documented vision against delivered work
description: Use a same-model sub-agent review to compare approved-doc claims with delivered behavior, evidence level, CSV state, validation evidence, and review log.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN all non-review issues before this row are closed THEN run same-model sub-agent review against source-specific claim and evidence checks; WHEN gaps or overstated claims are found THEN append follow-up issues and REVIEW-02; WHEN no gaps remain THEN close the CSV.
review_initial_requirements: Verify all prior non-review rows are closed before running this review.
review_regression_requirements: Run same-model sub-agent review against approved doc goals, non-goals, acceptance criteria, delivered diff, validation evidence, prior review logs, and these source-specific checks: (1) g==0 equivalence evidence is a real numeric comparison against the core code path, not mocked; (2) Gate 0, Gate 1, Gate 2 verdicts cite real dated artifacts and the recorded claim strength matches the Gate 2 exit; (3) fallback-safe and Prop 6 claims appear only if the pseudo-mass-frozen v2 implementation is verified in code; (4) the paper contains no remaining placeholders and respects the spec section 4.6 forbidden-claim list.
notes: review_kind:vision; review_agent:same-model-sub-agent; source_doc:docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-02-aaai27-fallback-safe-kernel-sprint-design.md
